accept: act create <title> --blocks <id> [--blocks <id>...] accepts one or more existing-issue ids; for each, an add_dep op is written with IssueID=<resolved-existing>, parent=<new>, edge_type=blocks.

All ops (create + each --blocks add_dep + each --blocked-by add_dep) are written and committed atomically; on any failure after the first op write, every staged op is rolled back so no half-state exists.
Direction is unambiguous from the flag name: --blocks means 'new ticket blocks <id>' (and consequently <id> appears in `act ready` only after the new ticket closes).

--blocks and --blocked-by are usable together on the same create call; duplicates within either list (resolving to the same full id) are folded; an id appearing on BOTH lists is a bad_flag (it would be a 2-cycle).
Self-loop guard: an id resolving to the issue being created is bad_flag, matching the existing --blocked-by guard.
Resolution errors (not_found, ambiguous_prefix) surface in the same error_envelope shape used by --blocked-by, with --blocks named in the message.
Help text on `act create --help` lists --blocks alongside --blocked-by with the direction primer from act dep add's help.

Tests cover: (a) repro of the inverse-direction trap that motivated the report; (b) happy path single --blocks; (c) repeatable + dedup; (d) mixed --blocks + --blocked-by; (e) error paths (not_found, ambiguous, self-loop, both-lists-share-id); (f) JSON envelope round-trip; (g) `act ready` correctly hides the existing target after the new issue lands.
payload.description:
Reported by Andrew 2026-05-15 after three independent agent reports in another project tripped the same trap.

PROBLEM
The "new ticket should block an existing ticket" pattern is a common agent move — e.g. a critic-review subagent files a follow-up that must land before the parent plan's fanout meta-ticket can run. Today this needs two non-atomic commands:

  act create "<new title>" [...]              # records new id, say act-NEW
  act dep add <existing-fanout> --blocked-by act-NEW   # OR --blocks (same edge)

`act create --blocked-by <id>` shipped in act-c26a but expresses the OPPOSITE direction (the new ticket is blocked BY the existing one). When agents reach for the create flag they're already on, they reliably misuse it: they write `act create --blocked-by <existing-fanout>`, which records "new ticket is blocked by fanout" — the inverse of intent. The new ticket is then correctly filtered out of `act ready`, the fanout stays unblocked, runs immediately, and the follow-up sits in the queue silently waiting for the very thing it was supposed to gate.

The second failure mode is even simpler: the agent does `act create` standalone, forgets the second `act dep add`, and the follow-up is orphaned with no edge at all.

Three independent agent reports in another project all hit one of these two failure modes.

DISCOVERED
External user report; no reproducer in this repo's backlog. Inverse to act-c26a (which shipped the --blocked-by direction for workflow C: "file a follow-up that the new ticket depends on"). The matching surface for workflow B ("file a follow-up that blocks the existing one") is missing.

PROPOSED FIX
Add `--blocks <id>` to `act create`, repeatable, symmetric in shape to --blocked-by. Each --blocks id resolves an existing issue and writes an add_dep op with IssueID=<existing>, parent=<new>, edge_type=blocks — mirroring how `act dep add --blocks` already resolves direction (see act-63a1). Same single-atomic-commit + rollback story as --blocked-by; same self-loop guard; same dedup. Make --blocks and --blocked-by mutually compatible on the same create call (an agent CAN legitimately file a ticket that is simultaneously blocked by some prereqs and blocks some downstream work).

RATIONALE
Single-command parity removes the inverse-direction trap entirely: the natural English "I'm filing a ticket that BLOCKS the fanout" maps to the literal flag the agent types. The two-step variant (`act create` then `act dep add --blocks`) keeps working unchanged; the new flag is purely additive.

ALTERNATIVES CONSIDERED (in user's report)
- Warn when a follow-up-style title pattern is used with no dep flags: weak signal, false positives, doesn't address the inverse-direction case.
- --related-to <id> shortcut that prompts for direction: prompts don't fit non-interactive agent invocation.

Symmetric flag wins on agent-ergonomics + parity with `act dep add`.
payload.title: act create --blocks <id> symmetric to --blocked-by (file-and-block workflow)
